messages.content: Look up security risks for Tradr-Project Tf Remapper version 1.1.1

messages.name: vector_database_retrieval
messages.content:
[CVE-2022-48217]
Severity: HIGH
CWE: NVD-CWE-noinfo
Description: The tf_remapper_node component 1.1.1 for Robot Operating System (ROS) allows attackers, who control the source code of a different node in the same ROS application, to change a robot's behavior. This occurs because a topic name depends on the attacker-controlled old_tf_topic_name and/or new_tf_topic_name parameter. NOTE: the vendor's position is "it is the responsibility of the programmer to make sure that only known and required parameters are set and unexpected parameters are not."
Known Vulnerable Software: cpe:2.3:a:tradr-project:tf_remapper:1.1.1:*:*:*:*:robot_operating_system:*:*
Mitigation: None
Vector DB Text: Application 'tf remapper' by tradr-project on robot operating system (version 1.1.1)
(Relevance Score: 0.7325)

---

[CVE-2019-25100]
Severity: CRITICAL
CWE: CWE-89
Description: A vulnerability was found in happyman twmap. It has been declared as critical. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the file twmap3/data/ajaxCRUD/pointdata2.php. The manipulation of the argument id leads to sql injection. Upgrading to version v2.9_v4.31 is able to address this issue. The identifier of the patch is babbec79b3fa4efb3bd581ea68af0528d11bba0c. It is recommended to upgrade the affected component. The identifier VDB-217645 was assigned to this vulnerability.
Known Vulnerable Software: cpe:2.3:a:twmap_project:twmap:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*
Mitigation: M1048, M1037, M1030, M1016, M1026, M1050, M1035, M1051
Vector DB Text: Application 'twmap' by twmap project (all versions)
(Relevance Score: 0.4262)

[CVE-2020-13133]
Severity: MEDIUM
CWE: CWE-79
Description: Tufin SecureChange prior to R19.3 HF3 and R20-1 HF1 are vulnerable to stored XSS. The successful exploitation requires admin privileges (for storing the XSS payload itself), and can exploit (be triggered by) unauthenticated users. All TOS versions with SecureChange deployments prior to R19.3 HF3 and R20-1 HF1 are affected. Vulnerabilities were fixed in R19.3 HF3 and R20-1 HF1
Known Vulnerable Software: cpe:2.3:a:tufin:securechange:r19-3:-:*:*:*:*:*:*
Mitigation: M1050, M1051, M1048, M1021, M1017
Vector DB Text: Application 'securechange' by tufin (version r19-3)
(Relevance Score: 0.3711)

[CVE-2023-0028]
Severity: MEDIUM
CWE: CWE-79
Description: Cross-site Scripting (XSS) - Stored in GitHub repository linagora/twake prior to 2023.Q1.1200+.


Known Vulnerable Software: cpe:2.3:a:linagora:twake:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

(Relevance Score: 0.3516)

Based on your query regarding security risks for Tradr-Project Tf Remapper version 1.1.1, one relevant vulnerability has been identified:

### Relevant Vulnerability:

**CVE-2022-48217**
- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Description:** The tf_remapper_node component of the Tradr-Project Tf Remapper version 1.1.1 for the Robot Operating System (ROS) is vulnerable. Attackers who control the source code of another node in the same ROS application can manipulate a robot's behavior. This occurs due to the dependency of topic names on attacker-controlled parameters, `old_tf_topic_name`, and `new_tf_topic_name`.
- **Exploitability:** This issue arises because topic names can be altered by unauthorized parameters, suggesting a lack of input validation or insufficient access control. It highlights the need for developers to enforce strict parameter validation, as per the vendor's guidance.
- **Implications:** Attackers could potentially hijack control flows within ROS applications, leading to unauthorized behavior by robots, which could be particularly dangerous depending on the robot's operational environment.
- **Mitigation:** The vendor indicates a need for developers to ensure rigorous parameter setting and check for any unexpected parameters.

### Conclusion:

This CVE is directly relevant to your query and should be taken seriously due to its potential impact on robot operations. It's crucial to review and tighten any security measures around parameter handling and trust boundaries within your application's architecture.

No further relevant vulnerabilities were found in the provided results. The other entries were either related to different software entirely or showed lower relevance scores, indicating they do not apply to Tradr-Project Tf Remapper version 1.1.1.
